title: Can you social engineer an AI? Plus: AI worms and the nonhuman identity problem
summary: IBM Security Intelligence podcast explores whether AI systems can be socially engineered like humans, examining a Meta/Instagram prompt injection attack, a self-replicating AI worm from the University of Toronto that can reason through networks, and organizational failures to audit nonhuman identities (NHIs) that cause data breaches.
writeup: An IBM Security Intelligence podcast episode examines whether AI systems can be manipulated through social engineering tactics used against people. It covers a prompt injection attack involving Meta and Instagram, a self-replicating AI worm developed at the University of Toronto that can reason its way through networks, and organizational failures to audit nonhuman identities (NHIs) that lead to data breaches. The throughline is that reasoning models and machine identities expand the attack surface beyond traditional human-targeted threats.
